papers.author: Craig (2009)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula is neural correlate of awareness; re-represents interoception; contains Von Economo neurons; fundamental to subjective feelings

papers.author: Olson et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: Temporal pole (BA38) is convergence zone binding perceptual inputs to visceral emotional responses; right=emotion/social, left=semantic

papers.author: Vogt (2005)
papers.key_finding: Four-region cingulate model: sACC/pACC (emotion/visceromotor), aMCC/pMCC (pain/skeletomotor), dPCC/vPCC, RSC. Replaces simplistic two-region model

papers.author: Kurth et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Meta-analysis of 1,768 experiments: four insular functional zones - sensorimotor (posterior), social-emotional (anterior-ventral), olfacto-gustatory (central), cognitive (anterior-dorsal)

papers.author: Allman et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Von Economo neurons (VENs) in FI and LA: large bipolar neurons in apes/humans only; more numerous in right hemisphere; destroyed early in FTD causing empathy loss

papers.author: Rolls (2020)
papers.key_finding: OFC is key emotion area: medial OFC represents reward, lateral OFC represents non-reward/punishment. Non-reward leads to sadness/depression

papers.author: Johansen-Berg et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Connectivity-based parcellation: pregenual ACC connects to mPFC; subgenual ACC connects to NAc, amygdala, hypothalamus, OFC. DBS contacts lie within subgenual region

papers.author: Bubb et al. (2017)
papers.key_finding: Updated Papez circuit as twin dorsal/ventral routes from hippocampus to cingulate/thalamus; multiply interconnected with topographic subsystems

papers.author: Aggleton (2010)
papers.key_finding: Hippocampus projects both directly and indirectly (via mammillary bodies/mammillothalamic tract) to anterior thalamus; disconnection produces memory deficits

papers.author: Catani & Thiebaut de Schotten (2008)
papers.key_finding: DTI tractography atlas of 26 reliably identified white matter tracts in MNI space; faithful to classical post-mortem descriptions

papers.author: Safadi et al. (2018)
papers.key_finding: ALIC segmented into 5 PFC-thalamic/brainstem channels: vmPFC/OFC, vlPFC, dlPFC, dACC, dmPFC. vlPFC projections abnormal in bipolar disorder

papers.author: Kamali et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: First DTI visualization of ventral amygdalofugal pathway in living humans; connects amygdala to hypothalamus, thalamus, basal forebrain, brainstem

papers.author: Di Paola et al. (2020)
papers.key_finding: AC transection impaired fear memory; BLA-NAc contralateral projections through AC encode negative valence; ipsilateral encode positive

papers.author: Jones et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Cingulum bundle has distinct subdivisions: anterior dorsal (attention/executive) and parahippocampal/temporal (episodic memory) with different microstructural properties

papers.author: Haber & Knutson (2010)
papers.key_finding: Key reward structures: ACC, OFC, ventral striatum, ventral pallidum, midbrain DA neurons. Gradient-like corticostriatal organization links anatomy to human imaging

papers.author: de Jong et al. (2022)
papers.key_finding: DRN 5-HT neuron subpopulations: anatomically defined projections to reward- vs anxiety-related structures are enriched in reward- vs anxiety-responsive cells respectively

papers.author: Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005)
papers.key_finding: LC-NE two modes: phasic (exploitation, task focus) and tonic (exploration, disengagement). LC receives direct input from ACC and OFC for utility monitoring

papers.author: Seeley et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: Identified salience network (dACC + orbital frontoinsular cortex + subcortical/limbic) dissociable from executive-control network. Prescan anxiety correlated with SN connectivity

papers.author: Menon (2011)
papers.key_finding: Triple network model: SN gates switching between DMN (internal) and CEN (external). Aberrant SN function underlies schizophrenia, depression, anxiety, dementia, autism

papers.author: Lindquist et al. (2012)
papers.key_finding: Little evidence for 1:1 emotion-brain region mapping; supports psychological constructionism: interacting general-purpose networks (not emotion-specific regions) active across emotion categories

papers.author: Kober et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: 162 studies: 6 distributed functional groups identified (medial/lateral frontal, posterior cortical x2, paralimbic, core limbic/brainstem). dmPFC (BA9/32) uniquely co-activated with both PAG and hypothalamus

papers.author: Etkin & Wager (2007)
papers.key_finding: Amygdala + insula hyperactivation in all anxiety disorders. Only PTSD showed hypoactivation of dACC/rACC/vmPFC (regulation regions)

papers.author: Phelps & LeDoux (2005)
papers.key_finding: Five amygdala functions: implicit emotional learning, emotional modulation of memory, emotional influences on attention/perception, emotion and social behavior, emotion inhibition/regulation

papers.author: Phillips et al. (2003)
papers.key_finding: Two-system model: ventral system (amygdala, insula, vSTR, vACC, vPFC) for identification/automatic regulation; dorsal system (hippocampus, dACC, dPFC) for effortful regulation

papers.author: Pessoa (2008)
papers.key_finding: Critiques locationist approach. Emotion-cognition interactions based on dynamic network coalitions. Brain hubs (high-connectivity regions) critical for information flow between "affective" and "cognitive" regions

papers.author: Barrett (2017)
papers.key_finding: Emotions are constructed predictions, not triggered reactions. Brain uses past experience (concepts) to predict sensory input and guide allostasis. Active inference framework

papers.author: Bota et al. (2015)
papers.key_finding: Network analysis of 16,000+ rat cortical connections: four asymmetric modules in core-shell arrangement. Entorhinal area has most association connections. U-shaped band of highly connected areas

papers.author: Wang et al. (2020)
papers.key_finding: CCFv3: 43 isocortical areas, 329 subcortical structures, 81 fiber tracts. Complete voxel-level annotation of entire mouse brain at 10um resolution

papers.author: Wu et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: MPOAGal neurons are necessary and sufficient for parental behavior in both sexes; optogenetic activation in virgin males suppresses aggression and induces pup grooming

papers.author: Kohl et al. (2018)
papers.key_finding: MPOAGal neurons coordinate motor, motivational, hormonal and social aspects of parenting by projecting to different brain regions depending on behavior type, sex and reproductive state

papers.author: Numan (2006)
papers.key_finding: MPOA depresses amygdala-medial hypothalamus aversion system and excites mesolimbic DA system to promote active maternal responses; integrated OXT and experience

papers.author: Brown et al. (2017)
papers.key_finding: Acute deletion of prolactin receptor in MPOA neurons results in profound deficits; all mothers abandon pups. Prolactin-responsive MPOA neurons form nexus of distributed parental network

papers.author: Kosfeld et al. (2005)
papers.key_finding: Intranasal oxytocin causes substantial increase in trust (trust game investments) not attributable to general risk-taking. Landmark human OXT study (note: replication challenges)

papers.author: Kirsch et al. (2005)
papers.key_finding: Intranasal OXT potently reduced amygdala activation and reduced amygdala-brainstem coupling for fear stimuli in humans

papers.author: Marlin et al. (2015)
papers.key_finding: OXT enhances left auditory cortex responses to pup calls by balancing excitation-inhibition; retrieval behavior requires left but not right auditory cortex

papers.author: Feldman (2012)
papers.key_finding: OXT system provides neurohormonal substrate for parental, romantic, and filial attachment; OXT shows stability within individuals and mutual influence among partners

papers.author: Feldman (2017)
papers.key_finding: Attachment built on OXT-DA integration in striatum; four systems of biobehavioral synchrony (behavior, autonomic, endocrine, neural) sustain parent-infant bonds

papers.author: Strathearn et al. (2009)
papers.key_finding: Securely attached mothers show greater VTA/striatum activation and higher peripheral OXT to own infant faces; insecure mothers show greater insula activation

papers.author: Strathearn et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Own infant smile activates VTA/SN, striatum, PFC; graded response: happy > neutral > sad faces. Dopamine reward regions track infant affect

papers.author: Noriuchi et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Own infant distress activates SN, caudate, thalamus, ACC, OFC, dmPFC; own infant joy activates OFC, insula, precuneus, PAG, putamen

papers.author: Swain et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: Review of parental brain neuroimaging: midbrain, basal ganglia, cingulate, amygdala, insula activated by own baby cry at 2-4 weeks postpartum

papers.author: Abraham et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: Primary-caregiving fathers show amygdala activation comparable to mothers; time in direct childcare linked to amygdala-STS connectivity. Common neural basis of maternal and paternal care

papers.author: Dolen et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: OXT acts in NAc core as social reinforcement signal; requires 5-HT1b receptor activation from dorsal raphe. Genetic deletion of OXTr in DRN abolishes social reward

papers.author: Champagne et al. (2001)
papers.key_finding: High-LG mothers have higher OXT receptor binding in MPOA, BNST, lateral septum, CeA. Maternal care quality programs OXT receptor expression epigenetically

papers.author: Weaver et al. (2004)
papers.key_finding: High LG/ABN alters offspring GR promoter methylation in hippocampus; effects emerge first week, persist to adulthood, reversed by cross-fostering. Causal link between maternal care and epigenetic programming

papers.author: Meaney & Szyf (2005)
papers.key_finding: Maternal behavior programs HPA axis via GR methylation; pharmacological reversal of chromatin modifications eliminates effects on stress responses

papers.author: Young & Wang (2004)
papers.key_finding: Mating activates VTA→NAc DA; concurrent OXT (females) or AVP (males) activation in NAc creates conditioned partner preference. Species-specific receptor distributions determine bonding capacity

papers.author: Keverne & Curley (2008)
papers.key_finding: Genomic imprinting of Peg3 gene affects hypothalamic development and maternal behavior; placental hormones program maternalism. Evolutionary perspective on epigenetic maternal care

papers.author: Panksepp & Watt (2011)
papers.key_finding: Depression arises from hyperactive PANIC/GRIEF + hypoactive SEEKING; opioid-serotonin interactions in dPAG mitigate distress; ultra-low-dose buprenorphine as antidepressant

papers.author: Eisenberger et al. (2003)
papers.key_finding: Social exclusion (cyberball) activates dACC more during exclusion vs inclusion; RVPFC activation inversely correlated with distress. ACC mediates RVPFC-distress relationship

papers.author: Eisenberger (2012)
papers.key_finding: Social attachment system borrowed pain computations; dACC and anterior insula underlie distress of both physical and social pain. Greater social distress = greater dACC/AI activity

papers.author: Kross et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Intense romantic rejection activates secondary somatosensory cortex and dorsal posterior insula (physical pain regions); first to show neural overlap in sensory pain areas

papers.author: Herman & Panksepp (1978)
papers.key_finding: Morphine reduces separation distress vocalizations dose-dependently (35-75%); naloxone increases distress vocalizations. Supports endorphin-addiction model of attachment

papers.author: Zubieta et al. (2003)
papers.key_finding: PET imaging reveals mu-opioid receptor activation in rostral ACC, ventral striatum, amygdala, thalamus during sustained sadness; reduced MOR activation correlated with higher distress

papers.author: Hsu et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Social rejection activates MOR system in ventral striatum, amygdala, midline thalamus, PAG (PET). Social acceptance also activates MOR but in different pattern. Opioid gating of social pain

papers.author: Machin & Dunbar (2011)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: endogenous opioids mediate warmth of social contact; withdrawal-like state during separation; primate and human evidence for opioid-attachment link

papers.author: Gundel et al. (2003)
papers.key_finding: Grief activates posterior cingulate, medial/superior frontal gyrus, cerebellum (both picture+word); dACC, insula, fusiform activated by picture of deceased

papers.author: O'Connor et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Complicated grief uniquely activates nucleus accumbens (correlated with yearning, not time since death); both CG and non-CG show pain-related dACC/insula. Reward system involvement in pathological grief

papers.author: Kalin & Shelton (2003)
papers.key_finding: Anxious rhesus monkeys show elevated basal cortisol and CSF CRH; CeA modulates behavioral inhibition; stable individual differences in anxiety-related brain/endocrine profiles

papers.author: Sbarra & Hazan (2008)
papers.key_finding: Attachment bonds involve mutual physiological regulation; separation causes dysregulation of sleep, HPA axis, immune function. Neural-endocrine framework for grief

papers.author: Najib et al. (2004)
papers.key_finding: Romantic grief activates insula, ACC, thalamus, caudate; recovery associated with decreased activation in these regions over time

papers.author: Davis et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: BNST mediates sustained anxiety (diffuse, unpredictable threat) vs CeA phasic fear (explicit threat); CRF in BNST drives sustained distress states

papers.author: Mackes et al. (2020)
papers.key_finding: Romanian orphans adopted into UK families show reduced total brain volume as adults; smaller temporal lobe volumes linked to lower IQ. Early separation/deprivation leaves lasting neural signatures

papers.author: Bosch et al. (2009)
papers.key_finding: Partner loss increases CRF mRNA in BNST and passive coping; CRF receptor antagonist blocks separation-induced behavioral changes. Direct evidence for CRF in grief-like behavior

papers.author: Mague et al. (2022)
papers.key_finding: Social separation and reunion produce distinct brain-wide electrophysiological signatures; individual differences in social motivation tracked by distributed neural dynamics

papers.author: Eisenberger et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Viewing photo of romantic partner reduces pain ratings and neural pain processing; partner images activate vmPFC safety signal. Attachment figures serve as neural analgesics

papers.author: Way et al. (2009)
papers.key_finding: G allele of OPRM1 A118G polymorphism associated with greater dACC/AI response to social rejection and higher dispositional rejection sensitivity. Genetic link between opioid system and social pain

papers.author: Kalin et al. (1995)
papers.key_finding: Opioid blockade disrupts mother-infant reunion behaviors; both mother and infant show altered contact patterns under naltrexone. Bidirectional opioid mediation of attachment

papers.author: Panksepp (2003)
papers.key_finding: Commentary on Eisenberger 2003: social pain co-opted physical pain circuits during evolution; opioids reduce both; ACC as shared substrate. Evolutionary perspective

papers.author: Hull & Dominguez (2007)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: MPOA dopamine facilitates male copulation; DA agonists in MPOA facilitate, antagonists impair sexual behavior. Testosterone aromatization to estradiol critical

papers.author: Dominguez & Hull (2005)
papers.key_finding: DA release in MPOA increases during precopulatory exposure to estrous female and during copulation; removes tonic inhibition for sensorimotor integration

papers.author: Balthazart & Ball (2006)
papers.key_finding: Brain aromatase rapidly converts testosterone to estradiol; neuroestrogen regulates sexual motivation within minutes. Aromatase activity modulated by neuronal activity

papers.author: Balthazart et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Central blockade of estrogen action or synthesis rapidly (within minutes) reduces sexual motivation without affecting copulatory performance. Dissociation of motivation and performance

papers.author: Young & Wang (2004)
papers.key_finding: Mating activates VTA→NAc DA; concurrent OXT/AVP activation creates conditioned partner preference. D2 activation facilitates, D1 activation prevents pair bond formation

papers.author: Aragona et al. (2006)
papers.key_finding: D2-like receptor activation in rostral NAc shell facilitates pair bond; D1-like activation prevents it. After bonding, D1 upregulation drives selective aggression toward novel mates

papers.author: Walum & Young (2018)
papers.key_finding: Review: pair bonding built on ancient circuits for individual recognition, social reward, territorial behavior, maternal nurturing. OXT-DA integration creates partner representation

papers.author: Resendez et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: Pair bonding upregulates D1 receptor and dynorphin mRNA in NAc; D1-mediated selective aggression requires kappa-opioid receptor activation. Bonding attenuates amphetamine reward

papers.author: Hamann et al. (2004)
papers.key_finding: Males show greater amygdala and hypothalamus activation to sexual stimuli even when females report equal arousal. Left amygdala sex difference larger than right

papers.author: Stoleru et al. (2012)
papers.key_finding: Consistent activation during sexual arousal: hypothalamus, anterior cingulate, insula, striatum, thalamus, claustrum, SN, cerebellum, occipitotemporal, orbitofrontal cortex

papers.author: Georgiadis & Kringelbach (2012)
papers.key_finding: Meta-analysis: sexual pleasure shares neural substrates with other hedonic experiences; orbitofrontal, insula, cingulate, striatum. Proposed Human Sexual Pleasure Cycle model

papers.author: Flanagan-Cato (2011)
papers.key_finding: VMH lordosis-controlling neurons receive estrogen-dependent dendritic remodeling; VMH→PAG→reticular formation circuit is sexually dimorphic

papers.author: Lin et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: VMHvl neurons drive aggression and mating behavior; optogenetic activation produces attack; overlapping but distinct populations for aggression vs mating

papers.author: Anderson (2012)
papers.key_finding: VMHvl contains intermingled neurons for mating and fighting; social context gates behavioral output. Optogenetic dissection of hypothalamic social behavior circuits

papers.author: Champagne et al. (2004)
papers.key_finding: Individual differences in NAc DA release correlate with maternal licking/grooming; high-LG dams show greater pup-evoked DA in NAc shell. Links CARE and reward systems

papers.author: Siviy & Panksepp (2011)
papers.key_finding: Executive play circuit: parafascicular thalamus, frontal cortex, striatum. Cholinergic, dopaminergic controls; opioids and cannabinoids positively modulate. All aversive neuropeptides reduce play

papers.author: Panksepp & Siviy (1987)
papers.key_finding: Parafascicular thalamus lesions reduce pinning by 73% and play solicitation; effect specific to play without compromising foraging. PFA as critical play hub

papers.author: Vanderschuren et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: play is intrinsically rewarding; NAc opioids and DA mediate reward; amygdala and PFC modulate; developmental role in social competence

papers.author: Achterberg et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: DA mediates motivational but NE does not; amphetamine increases conditioned place preference for play via DA; atomoxetine (NE reuptake inhibitor) no effect on play motivation

papers.author: Achterberg et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: Intra-NAc amphetamine increases play via D1 and D2 receptors; NAc DA blockade reduces play in highly motivated animals. NAc DA critical for play motivation

papers.author: Trezza & Vanderschuren (2008)
papers.key_finding: Indirect cannabinoid agonist URB597 enhances play (dependent on opioid and DA receptors); direct CB1 agonist WIN55,212-2 reduces play. eCB-opioid-DA interaction critical for play reward

papers.author: Trezza et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Intra-NAc morphine increases play; NAc opioid blockade prevents systemic morphine play enhancement. Mu-opioid receptors in NAc mediate positive value of social interaction

papers.author: Bell et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Play deprivation prevents normal dendritic pruning in mPFC; play-deprived rats have larger dendritic arbor in adulthood. Juvenile play shapes PFC development

papers.author: Himmler et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Play-experienced rats show greater mPFC cFos activation in novel social encounters as adults; play primes PFC for social responsiveness

papers.author: Burgdorf & Panksepp (2001)
papers.key_finding: Tickling (simulated play) evokes 50-kHz ultrasonic vocalizations (USVs) as marker of positive affect; rats approach tickling hand. 50-kHz USVs as biomarker of play-related joy

papers.author: Burgdorf et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: 50-kHz USVs driven by NAc DA; nucleus accumbens amphetamine elicits 50-kHz calls. Brain stimulation mapping identifies NAc as key site

papers.author: Ishiyama & Brecht (2016)
papers.key_finding: Tickling evokes intense activity in deep layers of trunk somatosensory cortex; microstimulation evokes USVs; anxiety suppresses responses. Tickling responses predict play-related neural activity

papers.author: Petrovich et al. (2022)
papers.key_finding: Play deprivation differentially affects inhibitory synapse development across mPFC subregions; region-specific effects on PV+ interneuron maturation

papers.author: Manduca et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: CB1 and mu-opioid receptors in NAc core interact to control social play; combined low-dose agonism produces synergistic enhancement

papers.author: Niesink & Van Ree (1989)
papers.key_finding: Opioid and DA systems independently contribute to social play; morphine enhances pinning via mu receptors; DA antagonists reduce play solicitation

quantitative_params.context: mus lesions reduce pinning by 73% and play solicitation; effect

papers.author: Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005)
papers.key_finding: LC-NE phasic mode driven by task-relevant decision outcomes (exploitation); tonic mode when utility wanes (exploration). LC receives ACC/OFC inputs monitoring task utility

papers.author: Sara & Bouret (2012)
papers.key_finding: LC activation in response to unexpected stimuli or conditioned relevant stimuli; NE facilitates sensory processing, cognitive flexibility, and offline memory consolidation. "Network reset" function

papers.author: Nogueira et al. (2022)
papers.key_finding: LC axon activity correlates with magnitude of unsigned visuomotor prediction errors; similar response profiles in motor and visual cortex. LC broadcasts prediction errors throughout dorsal cortex

papers.author: Breton-Provencher et al. (2022)
papers.key_finding: LC noradrenaline release in frontal and sensory cortex represents threat prediction error under uncertainty; temporally distinct from reward prediction error signals

papers.author: Lisman & Grace (2005)
papers.key_finding: Hippocampus detects novelty → subiculum → NAc → VP → VTA → DA release in hippocampus enhances LTP. Novelty-dependent memory consolidation via hippocampal-VTA loop

papers.author: Takeuchi et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: LC TH+ neurons project to hippocampus more than VTA; LC activation enhances memory via D1/D5 (not adrenergic) receptors. LC co-releases dopamine for novelty-based memory enhancement

papers.author: Kempadoo et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic activation of LC→hippocampus releases DA that enhances spatial memory; D1/D5 antagonism blocks effect. LC as dopamine source for hippocampal novelty signaling

papers.author: Blackford et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Amygdala responds to novel faces regardless of emotion; novelty response specific to human images (not scenes). Emotion not necessary for amygdalar novelty responses

papers.author: Blackford et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Both amygdala and hippocampus respond to novel common stimuli; only amygdala responds preferentially to novel unusual stimuli. Amygdala uniquely sensitive to unexpected events

papers.author: Corbetta & Shulman (2002)
papers.key_finding: Two attention networks: dorsal (IPS/FEF) for top-down selection; ventral (TPJ/VFC, right-lateralized) for stimulus-driven reorienting to unexpected salient events. Ventral as "circuit breaker"

papers.author: Corbetta et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Ventral attention network activated by unexpected stimuli across sensory modalities; TPJ integrates bottom-up salience with contextual expectations for reorienting

papers.author: Naatanen et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: MMN elicited by any discriminable change in auditory stimulation (100-250ms); pre-attentive index of deviance detection. Recent extensions to grammar and semantics

papers.author: Garrido et al. (2009)
papers.key_finding: MMN generated by temporo-frontal network; reflects predictive model violation. Two competing theories: adaptation hypothesis vs model-adjustment (predictive coding)

papers.author: Friston (2010)
papers.key_finding: Brain minimizes variational free energy (surprise); perception updates predictions, action changes predicted inputs. Prediction error minimization as fundamental brain principle

papers.author: Fouragnan et al. (2018)
papers.key_finding: Meta-analysis dissociates unsigned surprise (bilateral anterior insula, dACC) from signed prediction error (striatum for positive, amygdala for negative). Double dissociation of surprise and valence

papers.author: Feinstein et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Patient SM (bilateral amygdala lesion) shows no fear but preserved surprise, disgust, anger, sadness, happiness. Amygdala necessary for fear but not surprise experience

papers.author: Barto et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Formal distinction between novelty (unfamiliar) and surprise (unexpected given model); both trigger exploration but through different computational mechanisms

papers.author: Jepma et al. (2012)
papers.key_finding: Curiosity (anticipation of novel information) activates anterior insula and ACC; relief of curiosity activates striatum and hippocampus. Surprise-curiosity-learning cycle

quantitative_params.context: ange in auditory stimulation (100-250ms); pre-attentive index of devi
in auditory stimulation (100-250ms); pre-attentive index of devi

papers.author: Maren (2001)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: amygdala for cued fear, hippocampus for contextual fear; synaptic plasticity mapped

papers.author: Kim & Fanselow (1992)
papers.key_finding: Hippocampal lesions impair contextual but not cued fear; amygdala lesions impair both. Double dissociation

papers.author: Rogan, Staubli & LeDoux (1997)
papers.key_finding: First demonstration that fear conditioning induces LTP in auditory thalamic inputs to LA in vivo

papers.author: Rodrigues, Schafe & LeDoux (2004)
papers.key_finding: LA is key locus of Hebbian plasticity; NMDAR, CaMKII, MAPK cascades mediate acquisition and consolidation

papers.author: Johansen, Cain, Ostroff & LeDoux (2011)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review of molecular cascades (NMDA, AMPA trafficking, CREB) supporting synaptic plasticity in LA during fear conditioning

papers.author: Pape & Pare (2010)
papers.key_finding: Detailed review of coordinated network plasticity in amygdala: transient synaptic modifications stabilize during consolidation; extinction involves new learning, not erasure

papers.author: Duvarci & Pare (2014)
papers.key_finding: Fear depends on far more complex networks than initially envisioned; parallel inhibitory/excitatory circuits differentially recruited during expression vs extinction

papers.author: Ciocchi et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: CeL has ON (SOM+) and OFF (PKCdelta+) neurons; fear conditioning shifts balance to disinhibit CeM output

papers.author: Haubensak et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: PKCdelta+ CeL neurons inhibit CeM; reciprocal inhibition with PKCdelta- neurons; silencing PKCdelta+ cells increases freezing

papers.author: Li et al. (2013)
papers.key_finding: Fear conditioning induces bidirectional, cell-type-specific presynaptic plasticity in CeL; SOM+ activation necessary for fear recall and sufficient to drive fear

papers.author: Wolff et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: PV+ and SOM+ interneurons in BLA bidirectionally gate fear acquisition: PV+ excited by CS disinhibits dendrites; both inhibited by US boost postsynaptic responses

papers.author: Courtin et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: Fear expression driven by phasic inhibition of PFC PV+ interneurons; disinhibits projection neurons to BLA; resets local theta

papers.author: Dejean et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: Slow oscillation organizes dorsal mPFC assemblies into fear-generating circuits; behavior under tight temporal control by oscillation phase

papers.author: Quirk et al. (2002)
papers.key_finding: IL neurons fire to tone only during extinction recall; IL stimulation simulates extinction memory; consolidation potentiates IL activity

papers.author: Milad & Quirk (2012)
papers.key_finding: Review of extinction circuits: vmPFC/IL inhibits amygdala via ITCs; translational relevance for anxiety disorders; human-rodent parallels

papers.author: Sotres-Bayon et al. (2012)
papers.key_finding: BLA inputs excite PL projection neurons (drive fear); vHPC inputs activate PL interneurons (gate fear down). Hippocampus gates amygdala-based fear via PFC

papers.author: Herry et al. (2008)
papers.key_finding: Two distinct BA neuronal populations (fear neurons, extinction neurons) switch balance during fear/extinction; differentially connected to HPC and mPFC

papers.author: Likhtik et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: Theta-frequency entrainment of BLA by mPFC signals safety; gamma coupling during safety; disrupted theta-gamma coupling correlates with fear

papers.author: Tovote et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: CeA inhibitory projection to vlPAG produces freezing by disinhibiting excitatory vlPAG outputs to medullary pre-motor targets; interaction with flight circuits

papers.author: Mobbs et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: As virtual predator approaches, brain activity shifts from vmPFC to PAG; PAG activity correlates with subjective dread

papers.author: Johansen et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: US-evoked responses in both LA and PAG inhibited by expectation (prediction error); PAG inactivation impairs fear acquisition

papers.author: Tovote, Fadok & Luthi (2015)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: circuit-based approaches to fear/anxiety; cell-type-specific optogenetics reveals distinct defensive behavior circuits

papers.author: Davis et al. (2010)
papers.key_finding: Amygdala mediates phasic fear to explicit threats; BNST mediates sustained anxiety to diffuse/unpredictable threats via CRF; double dissociation

papers.author: Tully et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: NE enables LTP at thalamo-LA synapses under intact inhibition; beta-adrenergic mechanism; links emotional arousal to synaptic plasticity

papers.author: Tye et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic stimulation of BLA terminals in CeA: anxiolytic; inhibition: anxiogenic. Projection-specific, not soma-specific effects

papers.author: Beyeler et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: BLA-NAc neurons prefer reward cues; BLA-CeM neurons prefer aversive cues; valence encoded via divergent anatomical projections

papers.author: Klavir et al. (2017)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic modulation of BLA-mPFC connections bidirectionally modifies fear associations; demonstrates causal role of this projection

papers.author: Padilla-Coreano et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: vHPC-mPFC projection required for anxiety; inhibition disrupts theta synchrony and mPFC representations of aversion

papers.author: Blair, Schafe, Bauer, Rodrigues & LeDoux (2001)
papers.key_finding: Detailed cellular model: CS pathway LTP in LA via NMDAR; depotentiation during extinction; presynaptic and postsynaptic mechanisms

papers.author: Roozendaal & McGaugh (2011)
papers.key_finding: Glucocorticoids enhance memory consolidation via BLA; requires concurrent noradrenergic activation; stress hormone synergy

papers.author: Lin et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic stimulation of VMHvl causes male mice to attack females and objects; pharmacogenetic silencing inhibits inter-male aggression. Landmark study

papers.author: Lee et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: Esr1+ VMHvl neurons control graded social behavior: weak stimulation = sniffing/mounting; strong = attack. Continuous activity required during aggression

papers.author: Falkner et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: VMHvl drives flexible aggression-seeking behavior; learning-dependent activity changes link neutral behaviors to future aggression opportunities

papers.author: Falkner et al. (2020)
papers.key_finding: VMHvl-to-lPAG excitatory circuit transforms generalized social information into action-specific code time-locked to bite; lPAG inactivation causes aggression-specific deficits

papers.author: Hashikawa et al. (2017a)
papers.key_finding: Esr1+ VMHvl neurons control female aggression (not just male); optogenetic activation drives attack in lactating females

papers.author: Hashikawa et al. (2017b)
papers.key_finding: Review: VMHvl established as key aggression center; overlapping mating/aggression circuits with competitive dynamics

papers.author: Hsu et al. (2023)
papers.key_finding: Review: reproductive behavior control column (MPN, VMHvl, PMv) essential for all social behaviors; VMHvl shows largest, fastest activity increase at attack onset

papers.author: Hong, Kim & Anderson (2014)
papers.key_finding: MeA GABAergic neurons promote aggression and social behaviors; neighboring glutamatergic neurons promote self-grooming; mutual inhibition creates behavioral seesaw

papers.author: Nelson & Trainor (2007)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: hypothalamic/limbic areas facilitate aggression; frontal cortex inhibits. Serotonin, vasopressin, NO modulate. Gene-environment interactions

papers.author: Siegel & Victoroff (2009)
papers.key_finding: Neural circuits for emotional reactivity, emotion regulation, and cognitive control involved in aggression; defensive rage vs predatory attack distinction

papers.author: Miczek et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: 5-HT release regulation via 5-HT1A/1B autoreceptors and GABAergic/glutamatergic inputs are candidate mechanisms for transition from adaptive to escalated aggression

papers.author: Takahashi et al. (2011)
papers.key_finding: Different 5-HT receptor subtypes have opposing roles: 5-HT1A/1B/2A/2C activation in mesocorticolimbic areas reduces aggression; same receptors in mPFC/septum can increase it

papers.author: de Almeida et al. (2005)
papers.key_finding: Review of DA, 5-HT, GABA interactions in escalated aggression; DA facilitates aggression via mesolimbic system; GABA modulates in hypothalamus

papers.author: Coccaro et al. (2007)
papers.key_finding: IED patients show increased amygdala and decreased OFC reactivity to angry faces; diminished amygdala-OFC connectivity

papers.author: Rosell & Siever (2015)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review: amygdala-prefrontal circuitry, serotonin system, dopamine, vasopressin, cortisol/testosterone in aggression

papers.author: Archer (2006)
papers.key_finding: Weak, inconsistent association between testosterone and aggression in adults; challenge hypothesis partially supported; testosterone rises with competition

papers.author: Mehta & Josephs (2010)
papers.key_finding: Testosterone positively related to dominance ONLY when cortisol is low; high cortisol blocks testosterone-aggression link

papers.author: Kruk et al. (2004)
papers.key_finding: Mutual positive feedback between HPA axis and hypothalamic aggression area; corticosterone injection facilitates hypothalamic aggression

papers.author: Haller (2018)
papers.key_finding: Lateral hypothalamus strongly activated in glucocorticoid-deficient aggressive animals; drives violent attack on vulnerable body parts (head, throat, belly)

papers.author: Golden et al. (2016)
papers.key_finding: GABAergic BF-to-LHb projection bidirectionally controls aggression valence: silencing abolishes aggression CPP; activation promotes CPP in non-aggressors

papers.author: Flanigan et al. (2020)
papers.key_finding: Orexin from lateral hypothalamus activates GAD2+ LHb neurons via OxR2; promotes aggression and conditioned place preference for aggression

papers.author: Haller & Kruk (2006)
papers.key_finding: Distinguishes normal from abnormal aggression; etiological factors in aggression-related psychopathologies; implications for treatment

papers.author: Takahashi et al. (2014)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic activation of excitatory mPFC neurons inhibits inter-male aggression; OFC activation has no effect; mPFC as specific top-down brake

papers.author: Gregg & Siegel (2003)
papers.key_finding: 5-HT1A agonist in medial hypothalamus suppresses PAG-elicited rage (dose-dependent); 5-HT2C agonist facilitates rage

papers.author: Siegel & Edinger (1983)
papers.key_finding: Classical demonstration that limbic system (amygdala, septum, hippocampus) modulates hypothalamic attack; amygdala has both facilitatory and inhibitory effects

papers.author: Berridge(1998)
papers.key_finding: DA = "wanting" (incentive salience), NOT "liking"; opioids mediate hedonic pleasure

papers.author: Bromberg-Martin(2010)
papers.key_finding: Two DA neuron types: value-coding (excited by reward, inhibited by aversion) and salience-coding (excited by both)

papers.author: Cohen(2012)
papers.key_finding: DA neurons = RPE (Type I); GABA neurons = expected reward signal (Type II). Optogenetic tagging confirmed

papers.author: Bayer & Glimcher(2005)
papers.key_finding: DA neuron firing quantitatively predicted by TD-model RPE for positive prediction errors

papers.author: Dabney(2020)
papers.key_finding: DA neurons encode full reward distribution (not just mean); distributional RL in the brain

papers.author: Haber & Knutson(2010)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive cortical-basal ganglia reward circuit: ACC, OFC, ventral striatum, ventral pallidum, VTA DA

papers.author: Ikemoto(2007)
papers.key_finding: Posteromedial VTA projects to medial NAc shell; lateral VTA projects to NAc core and lateral shell

papers.author: Morales & Margolis(2017)
papers.key_finding: VTA contains DA, GABA, glutamate neurons with distinct connectivity; some co-release multiple transmitters

papers.author: Floresco(2015)
papers.key_finding: NAc integrates frontal/temporal inputs; shell vs core subserve distinct functions in action selection and motivation

papers.author: Roitman(2005)
papers.key_finding: NAc neurons inhibited by sucrose (75%), excited by quinine (75%); segregated microcircuit for valence

papers.author: Salamone & Correa(2012)
papers.key_finding: NAc DA mediates effort, behavioral activation, and approach behavior; NOT hedonic pleasure per se

papers.author: Berridge(2009)
papers.key_finding: Hedonic hotspot in NAc medial shell (1 mm^3): mu-opioid stimulation increases liking; DA increases wanting across all NAc

papers.author: Floresco(2003)
papers.key_finding: Pallidal inputs regulate population activity (tonic DA); pedunculopontine inputs regulate burst firing (phasic DA)

papers.author: Padoa-Schioppa & Assad(2006)
papers.key_finding: OFC neurons encode subjective value of offered/chosen goods, independent of visuospatial or motor factors

papers.author: Rolls & Grabenhorst(2008)
papers.key_finding: OFC represents reward value of primary reinforcers (taste, touch, smell); provides common currency for comparison

papers.author: Gruber(2014)
papers.key_finding: High curiosity enhances midbrain + NAc activity; curiosity-driven memory benefits depend on VTA-hippocampus connectivity

papers.author: Duzel(2010)
papers.key_finding: Hippocampus-VTA-NAc loop for novelty detection; DA promotes exploration and memory encoding for novel stimuli

papers.author: Nair-Roberts(2008)
papers.key_finding: VTA composition: ~60% DA, ~35% GABA, ~2-3% glutamate neurons by stereological count

papers.author: Watabe-Uchida(2012)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive monosynaptic input mapping to VTA DA neurons using rabies tracing; anterior cortex to VTA to NAc circuit

papers.author: Nestler & Carlezon(2006)
papers.key_finding: NAc and VTA contribute to anhedonia, reduced motivation in depression; CREB/dynorphin/BDNF in VTA-NAc

papers.author: Robinson & Berridge(1993)
papers.key_finding: Repeated drug use sensitizes mesolimbic DA = pathological wanting without increased liking

papers.author: Panksepp & Watt(2011)
papers.key_finding: Depression = overactive PANIC/GRIEF + diminished SEEKING; loss of DA-mediated enthusiasm

papers.author: Grace(2007)
papers.key_finding: Hippocampal input drives DA population activity; PFC gates phasic responses; model for goal-directed behavior

papers.author: Mayberg(1999)
papers.key_finding: Sadness = limbic increases (sgACC, insula) + cortical decreases (dlPFC, parietal); depression recovery shows reverse

papers.author: Drevets(1997)
papers.key_finding: sgPFC grey matter volume reduced 39-48% in bipolar/unipolar depression; decreased metabolism

papers.author: Ressler & Mayberg(2007)
papers.key_finding: Review of emotion circuit abnormalities in depression; DBS, TMS, VNS as circuit-based therapies

papers.author: Johansen-Berg(2008)
papers.key_finding: sgACC connects to NAc, amygdala, hypothalamus, OFC; distinct from pregenual ACC connectivity

papers.author: Matsumoto & Hikosaka(2007)
papers.key_finding: LHb neurons excited by no-reward cues, inhibited by reward cues; LHb stimulation inhibits DA neurons

papers.author: Zubieta(2003)
papers.key_finding: Social rejection activates mu-opioid system in ventral striatum, amygdala, thalamus, PAG; reduces social pain

papers.author: Hamilton(2015)
papers.key_finding: Increased DMN-sgACC functional connectivity in MDD; predicts rumination levels; sgACC integrates self-referential + withdrawal

papers.author: Craig(2009)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula re-represents interoception; correlates with all subjective feelings including sadness

papers.author: Harrison(2009)
papers.key_finding: Inflammation-induced fatigue predicted by insula + ACC activity; sgACC reactivity associated with mood changes

papers.author: Krishnan & Nestler(2008)
papers.key_finding: Maladaptive stress-induced neuroplastic changes in specific circuits; molecular basis of susceptibility vs resilience

papers.author: Nestler & Carlezon(2006)
papers.key_finding: Anhedonia involves VTA-NAc dysfunction; CREB, dynorphin, BDNF, Clock in depression pathophysiology

papers.author: Tye(2013)
papers.key_finding: Optogenetic inhibition of VTA DA = depression symptoms; phasic activation rescues chronic stress-induced depression

papers.author: Duman & Aghajanian(2012)
papers.key_finding: Chronic stress reduces synapses in PFC; ketamine rapidly increases synaptogenesis via mTOR and BDNF

papers.author: Kupfer(2012)
papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review of depression neurobiology including imaging, genetics, biomarkers

papers.author: Holtzheimer & Mayberg(2011)
papers.key_finding: Review of DBS approaches; sgACC target most studied; distinct therapeutic response phases

papers.author: Panksepp & Watt(2011)
papers.key_finding: Depression = overactive separation distress (PANIC) + hypoactive reward exploration (SEEKING)

papers.author: Gusnard(2001)
papers.key_finding: mPFC highest baseline metabolism; self-referential processing hub; decreases during goal-directed tasks

papers.author: Keedwell(2005)
papers.key_finding: Depressed patients show increased amygdala/parahippocampal response to sad faces; reduced to happy faces

papers.author: Vytal & Hamann(2010)
papers.key_finding: Sadness associated with sgACC activation; fear with amygdala; disgust with insula; distinct but overlapping

grey matter volume reduced 39-48% in bipolar/unipolar depressio

papers.author: Phillips(1997)
papers.key_finding: Disgust faces activate anterior insula and limbic cortico-striatal-thalamic circuit; NOT amygdala

papers.author: Adolphs(2003)
papers.key_finding: Patient with bilateral insula damage: uniformly impaired disgust recognition from both static and action stimuli

papers.author: Sprengelmeyer(1998)
papers.key_finding: Functional imaging confirms basal ganglia + insula involvement in facial disgust recognition

papers.author: Chapman(2009)
papers.key_finding: Gustatory distaste, core disgust, and moral unfairness all activate levator labii muscle (oral rejection response)

papers.author: Craig(2009)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula = interoceptive hub; correlates with all subjective emotional feelings including disgust

papers.author: Tybur(2009)
papers.key_finding: Three disgust domains: pathogen, sexual, moral; each with distinct sensitivity profiles

papers.author: Moll(2005)
papers.key_finding: Core disgust: medial OFC; moral disgust: anterior OFC + anterior SFG; overlapping and distinct regions

papers.author: Small(2003)
papers.key_finding: Unpleasant taste: left dorsal anterior insula; pleasant taste: right caudolateral OFC; intensity: middle insula

papers.author: Carter(2015)
papers.key_finding: PBN CGRP neurons necessary and sufficient for establishing CTA; optogenetic activation induces CTA

papers.author: Mataix-Cols(2008)
papers.key_finding: Disgust sensitivity positively correlates with anterior insula, putamen, vlPFC, dACC activation to disgusting images

papers.author: Miller(2021)
papers.key_finding: Area postrema molecular atlas: specific cell types for emesis; NTS-PBN-insula ascending pathway

papers.author: Harrison(2010)
papers.key_finding: Nauseating disgust: right insula + stomach contraction; body-boundary disgust: left insula + cardiac parasympathetic

papers.author: Cisler(2009)
papers.key_finding: Disgust sensitivity linked to OCD contamination fears; higher difficulty regulating disgust emotions

papers.author: Krolak-Salmon(2003)
papers.key_finding: Intracranial EEG in epilepsy patients: disgust-specific N200 response in ventral anterior insula

papers.author: Jabbi(2008)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula active for tasting, watching, and imagining disgust; connectivity differs by context

papers.author: Sambataro(2006)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula preferentially activated by disgust vs other negative emotions

papers.author: Stark(2007)
papers.key_finding: Anterior insula and amygdala differentially respond to disgust and fear stimuli; insula more for disgust

papers.author: Critchley(2004)
papers.key_finding: Right anterior insula activity correlates with interoceptive accuracy; mediates awareness of bodily states
